element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player given time to build before patients arrive
Available at game start; foundation of patient flow
Each role requires specific skills and management
Required after GP diagnosis; unlocks treatment pathways
Must be researched; doctors need specialist skills
Unlocks new rooms and improves existing ones over time
Failure results in patient death; creates ongoing tension
Enables financial optimization and patient flow control
Sudden events; patients die if not cured in time limit
Can cover up or face health inspector fine; reputation damage
Optional events; reward-based (cash bonus or reputation)
Environmental hazard; player shoots with cursor

typical_tension_triggers: Patients arriving faster than diagnosis/treatment capacity
Insufficient specialized doctors for treatment rooms
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension with multiple overlapping pressure systems; early game (safe building phase) gives way to continuous management of patient flow, maintenance, and events; late game introduces cascading emergencies and epidemics that spike tension sharply

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Decorative items (benches, fire extinguishers, plants) in corridors; non-essential but improve aesthetic and patient satisfaction

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like; player given time to build hospital before patients arrive; basic rooms available; focus on fundamental mechanics (room placement, staff hiring, patient flow)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of specialized diagnosis and treatment requirements; research unlocks new rooms; maintenance becomes critical; first emergency/epidemic events; financial pressure increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multiple cascading systems requiring simultaneous management; frequent emergencies and epidemics; staff management becomes complex; reputation becomes volatile; computer rivals create competitive pressure
notable_spikes: First epidemic with health inspector threat (reputation damage consequence)
Introduction of machine maintenance with explosion consequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level begins with a grace period where player constructs hospital infrastructure before patient flow begins; allows strategic planning and system establishment

Core gameplay loop involves managing diagnosis → specialized evaluation → treatment pipeline; pressure increases as patient queue length grows; requires balancing room availability with staff specialization
Emergencies and epidemics cluster together, creating moments of high stress followed by recovery periods; forces dynamic reallocation of resources
Specialized treatment machines require regular handyman maintenance; neglect creates explosive failure consequences; creates ongoing tension separate from patient flow management
New rooms unlock gradually through research; timing of unlocks gates access to treating new diseases; creates strategic research prioritization decisions
